questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A train of length 150 m is moving towards north with velocity 10 m/s. A parrot is flying with velocity 5 m/s towards south direction parallel to railway track. The time taken by the parrot to cross the train is:
questions.explanation: From hint: Time of crossing = 150 / (10+5) = 150/15 = 10 sec.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two trains each of length 50 m are moving in opposite direction towards each other on parallel lines with speeds 10 m/s each. Then the time of crossing will be:
questions.explanation: From answer key (2.c). The hint gives 4 sec which does not match options; key says C.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: To a person going east in a car with velocity of 25 km/hr, a train appears to move towards north with a velocity of 25√3 km/hr. The actual velocity of the train will be:
questions.explanation: From hint: V_T = √(25² + (25√3)²) = 50 km/hr.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two cars are moving in same direction with the same speed of 30 km/hr. They are separated by a distance 5 km. A third car moving in opposite direction meets these two cars at an interval of 4 minutes. The speed of the third car will be?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A bird is flying towards south with a velocity of 40 km/hr and a train is moving with velocity 40 km/hr towards east. What is the velocity of the bird w.r.t an observer in train?
questions.explanation: From hint: V_bt = V_b - V_t = 40 south - 40 east = 40√2 km/hr S-W.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two cars each of length 20 m are moving in such a way that their time of crossing is 2 sec and their time of overtaking is 4 sec. Then speeds of the cars will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car X is moving with velocity 80 km/hr towards North-east direction and another car Y moving with velocity 60 km/hr towards south-east direction. Then the angle made by velocity of car X relative to car Y from North will be:
questions.explanation: From hint: tan δ = 1/7 ⇒ δ = tan⁻¹(1/7).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Wind is blowing west to east. Two trains moving with same speed along two parallel tracks in opposite direction have the steam track of one doubled than other. The speed of each train will be:
questions.explanation: From hint: x + y = 2(x - y) ⇒ x = 3y.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A police patrol Jeep is pursuing a car on straight level road. The jeep and the car are moving with speeds of 60 km/hr and 72 km/hr respectively. The patrol jeep fires a bullet when the car is 200 m from it. If the speed of bullet is 500 m/s, then the relative separation between the two vehicles when the bullet strikes the car will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two cars A and B are moving in the same direction with velocities V₁ and V₂ (V₁ > V₂). When the separation between the cars is 'd', the driver of A applies brakes and produces constant retardation 'a'. Then there will be no collision if:
questions.explanation: From hint: For no collision, d > (V₁ - V₂)²/(2a).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car travelling at 60 km/hr overtakes another car travelling at 42 km/hr. Assuming each car to be 5 m long, find the time taken during the overtake.
questions.explanation: From hint: Relative speed = 18 km/hr = 5 m/s, distance = 10 m, time = 2 sec.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In the above question, the total road distance used for the overtake is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A police jeep is chasing a culprit going on a motorbike. The motorbike crosses a turning at a speed of 72 km/hr. The jeep follows it at a speed of 90 km/hr, crossing the turning ten seconds later than the bike. Assuming they travel at constant speeds, how far from the turning will the jeep catch up with the bike?
questions.explanation: From hint: Distance = 1000 m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Stationary man observes that the rain is falling vertically downward. When he starts running with a velocity of 12 km/hr he observes that the rain is falling at an angle 60° with the vertical. The actual velocity of rain is:
questions.explanation: From hint: V_r = 12 / tan60° = 12/√3 = 4√3 km/hr.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car is moving on a road and rain is falling vertically, then select the correct answer.
questions.optionA: The rain will strike the hind screen only
questions.optionB: The rain will strike the front screen only
questions.optionC: The rain will strike both the screens
questions.optionD: The rain will not strike any screen
questions.explanation: Standard result: rain appears to come from front, so front screen only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A boatman wants to take his boat to a point just opposite on the other bank of river. Then
questions.optionC: he should incline to the bank upstream
questions.optionD: he should incline to the bank downstream
questions.explanation: To counteract drift, he must row at an angle upstream.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A man is walking due east at the rate of 4 km/hr and rain appears to him to be falling vertically. When he doubles his speed it appears to be falling at an angle of 45° with the vertical. Then the actual speed and direction of the rain will be:
questions.explanation: From hint: cot α = (2-1) cot45° = 1 ⇒ α=45°, V_r = 4/sin45° = 4√2 km/hr.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A boat which has a speed of 5 km/hr in still water crosses a river of width 1 km along the shortest possible path in 15 minutes. The velocity of the river water will be:
questions.explanation: From hint: 1/4 = 1/√(25-u²) ⇒ u=3 km/hr.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A river is flowing from west to east at a speed of 5 m/min. A man on the south bank of the river capable of swimming at 10 m/min in still water wants to cross the river in shortest time. In which direction should he start swimming?
questions.explanation: For shortest time, swim perpendicular to flow, i.e., due north.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A boat takes 2 hr to travel 8 km and returning back in still water. If a river is flowing with velocity 4 km/hr, then time taken by boat to travel 8 km and returning back in upstream of the river will be?
questions.explanation: From answer key (20.b). Still water speed = 8 km/hr, upstream time = 8/(8-4)=2 hr, downstream = 8/(8+4)=2/3 hr, total = 2 hr 40 min.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A boat takes 2 hr to travel 8 km and returning back in still water. If a river is flowing with velocity 4 km/hr, then time taken by boat to travel 8 km and returning back in upstream of the river will be?
questions.explanation: Same as Q20. From key 21.b.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A person aiming to reach directly opposite point on the other bank of a river is capable of swimming with velocity 0.5 m/s. His head makes 120° with the direction of flow. Then the flow velocity of water in the river will be:
questions.explanation: From hint: u = V sin30° = 0.5×0.5 = 0.25 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A man swims 3 km in 3 hr in still water, while a log floating downstream covers a distance of 1 km during this time. How many km will the man swim upstream in 3 hrs?
questions.explanation: From hint: Upstream distance = (1 - 1/3)×3 = 2 km.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A man can swim with a velocity of 5 km/hr in still water, jumps into a river of width 1 km flowing with velocity 3 km/hr perpendicular to the direction of flow. If he takes total of 20 minutes to reach directly opposite point on the other bank, then his velocity on the other bank will be?
questions.explanation: From hint: Speed = 0.6 / (2/15) = 4.5 km/hr.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two particles A and B are connected by a rigid rod AB. The rod slides along perpendicular rails as shown. The velocity of A to the left is 20 m/s. What is the velocity of B when angle α = 60°?
questions.explanation: From hint: V_B = 20/√3 ≈ 11.55? But key says A (5.8). The hint gives V_B = 20/√3 ≈ 11.55, not 5.8. Possibly different interpretation. Using V_B = V_A cot α = 20 cot60° = 20/1.732 = 11.55. However answer key indicates A. I retain the key.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In the arrangement shown, ends P and Q of an unstretchable string move downwards with uniform speed u. Pulleys A and B are fixed. The mass M moves with a velocity of:
questions.explanation: From hint: V_m = u / cos θ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For a cyclist running 5 m/s due west, the rain appears to fall vertically. When he increases his speed to 17 m/s due west, the rain appears to fall at an angle of 45° with the vertical. Find the actual speed of the rain.
questions.explanation: From hint: V_r = 5 / (5/13) = 13 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An airplane pilot wishes to fly due north. A wind of 30 km/hr is blowing towards west. If the flying speed of the plane is 360 km/hr, then what is the speed of the plane over the ground?
questions.explanation: From hint: V_p = √(360² - 30²) = 359.6 km/hr.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In flying an airplane, a pilot wants to maintain a velocity of 500 km/hr eastward with respect to ground. A wind of 40 km/hr is blowing south. What velocity (magnitude and direction) must the pilot maintain with respect to wind?
questions.explanation: From hint: V_pw = √(500²+40²) ≈ 502 km/hr, direction tan⁻¹(40/500) N of E.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Three particles are placed at the corners of an equilateral triangle of side 'a'. Each particle moves with constant speed 'v' in such a way that 1→2, 2→3, and 3→1. After what time will they meet?
questions.explanation: From hint: t = 2a/(3v).
